---
title: "Day 5 — Boolean Combinations"
subtitle: "Combine separate checks into one decision — and know which one controls it"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Generic booleans (a, b, lower_ok, upper_ok).
Core question: "Combine the checks — and name the controlling condition."
============================================================ -->

## The three connectors

| Connector | Rule | Passes when |
|---|---|---|
| `and` | **all-required** | every part is `True` |
| `or`  | **at-least-one** | any part is `True` |
| `not` | **reverse** | flips a Boolean (for follow-up logic) |

```{python}
#| eval: true
a = True
b = False
a and b   # False: one required part failed
a or b    # True:  at least one part passed
not b     # True:  b failed -> follow-up may be needed
```

## One false part sinks an `and`

<!-- TODO: the "controlling false condition" idea — name WHICH check failed,
     not just the final False. -->

::: {.trap-band}
⚠️ **Watch out** — using `or` for an all-required rule is unsafe: it can report "ready" when only **one** check passed and hide a real failure.
:::

## Building readiness from separate evidence

<!-- TODO: lower_ok / upper_ok -> flow_ok = lower_ok and upper_ok,
     then combine with approval/setup/retry checks. Keep each a named Boolean. -->

## Condition ledger

<!-- TODO: reusable table students fill in before writing the final expression. -->

| Check (name) | Source | Value | Connector | Effect on result |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `lower_ok` | value ≥ low | … | `and` | … |
| `upper_ok` | value ≤ high | … | `and` | … |
| … | … | … | … | … |

## Explicit label checks carry forward

<!-- TODO: still use label == "reviewed", not bool(label) (Day 3). -->

## Recompute, don't hand-edit

<!-- TODO: if an input changes, RE-DERIVE the downstream Booleans rather than
     flipping the final variable by hand. -->

::: {.check-band}
✅ **Non-mutating check** — evaluate `record_ready` (or any Boolean) as a bare expression to read the decision; it stores nothing.
:::

## Show your evidence

::: {.evidence-band}
🔎 **Show your evidence** — *trace* each Boolean; *debug* an unsafe `or`; when the answer is `False`, *explain* by naming the single controlling condition.
:::
